article_meta.title: New York Playbook POLITICO's must-read briefing informing the daily conversation among knowledgeable New Yorkers New Yor

reasoning: The model systematically violates the attribution rule by flagging quoted text from sources as author bias. All four flagged items are from quoted or attributed sources: (1) Sen. Gounardes' interview statements, (2) Sen. Gounardes' interview statements, (3) John Kaehny's quote, and (4) John Avlon's launch video quote. None of these are the author's own prose. The model also uses non-standard bias type labels ('Loaded Language', 'Assertion as Fact', 'Negative Framing', 'Partisan Labeling') that don't match the provided list. Meanwhile, there are some potential author-level bias instances that were missed, such as 'FEED FIGHT' as a sensationalized section header, 'evading the prohibition' as loaded framing in the Carone story, and 'Avlon's history isn't true blue though' as author editorial commentary.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All four flagged items are from quoted sources or attributed statements: Gounardes' interview (items 1 and 2), Kaehny's quote (item 3), and Avlon's launch video quote (item 4). Zero items come from author prose. This is a systematic attribution rule violation.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All four flagged items are attribution rule violations - none represent genuine author-level bias. Every single flagged item is a false positive.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed potential author-level bias such as 'evading the prohibition against lobbyist donating' (loaded framing by author), 'Avlon's history isn't true blue though' (author editorial commentary/spin), and the overall structural slant in the Carone story. These are more subtle but some are defensible instances.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The model used labels not in the provided list: 'Loaded Language', 'Assertion as Fact', 'Negative Framing', and 'Partisan Labeling' are all non-standard. The valid list includes specific types like 'Word Choice', 'Opinion Statements Presented as Fact', 'Sensationalism/Emotionalism', etc.
